title: Voice In, Visuals Out: The Agony and the Ecstasy - Allen Pike, Forestwalk Labs
summary: Allen Pike discusses voice-in, visuals-out as a superior AI UX paradigm, following Andrej Karpathy's thesis. The talk covers why this approach works for LLM-powered products, user delight factors, and techniques for achieving low latency in responsive AI agents.
writeup: Allen Pike of Forestwalk Labs argues for "voice-in, visuals-out" as a UX paradigm for LLM-powered products, building on Andrej Karpathy's thesis that speaking input paired with visual output beats voice-only or text-only interfaces. The talk explains why this pairing suits AI agents, what drives user delight, and the techniques needed to keep latency low enough for responsiveness. The takeaway is a concrete design pattern (accept spoken input, render results visually) along with the latency engineering required to make it feel responsive.
